content:
## Autonomous Characterisation Tests

When a new invariant enters the spec, there is a delta between the spec and the implementation — that delta is work. If an agent builds something but no tests are added, the eventual desire is that autonomous agents rectify this by writing characterisation tests of decisions that have been made. The system should detect both "specified but not built" and "built but not tested" states. [[source:811302f6-0be7-435c-b844-910cc9a21b67/29]]

provenance.raw_agent: The two cases you're describing: New invariant, not yet implemented — the spec has moved ahead of the code. Built but not tested — the code exists but the characterisation test doesn't.
provenance.raw_user: I'm thinking about what happens when a new invariant enters the spec. It will be implemented or not. There is potentially a delta that is the work that needs doing. Also, If I ask an agent to build something, but I don't add tests, my eventual desire is that this situation is rectified by autonomous agents writing characterisation tests of decisions that have been made
